metadata.title: The Photoelectric Effect
metadata.notes: Concept 2 of chapter 1 — the film the UV outro promised. GRAPHIC ~53% accepted deliberately (three impossible facts + resolution = evidence section).
metadata.description_blurb: In 1887 Heinrich Hertz proved light is a wave — and noticed, the same year, the experiment that would break the wave theory. Three impossible facts, one patent clerk, and the equation that made light come apart in chunks.

beats.narration_text: In 1887, Heinrich Hertz proved light is a wave — the crowning triumph of classical physics. The same year, he noticed something that would destroy it.
beats.tts_normalized_text: In 1887, Heinrich Hertz proved light is a wave — the crowning triumph of classical physics. The same year, he noticed something that would destroy it.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Hertz (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Heinrich Hertz portrait or spark-gap apparatus, 1880s; set shot.focus on his face / the gap

beats.narration_text: Ultraviolet light striking metal knocks out sparks. Simple to see, impossible to explain. This is the experiment that broke the wave theory of light.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Ultraviolet light striking metal knocks out sparks. Simple to see, impossible to explain. This is the experiment that broke the wave theory of light.

card.title: THE PHOTOELECTRIC EFFECT
card.sub: how light came apart in chunks

beats.narration_text: By 1902, Philipp Lenard had shown the sparks were electrons. By 1914, Robert Millikan had measured every feature of them. The data said three impossible things.
beats.tts_normalized_text: By 1902, Philipp Lenard had shown the sparks were electrons. By 1914, Robert Millikan had measured every feature of them. The data said three impossible things.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Lenard (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Philipp Lenard at apparatus, or a cathode-ray-era laboratory, circa 1900

beats.narration_text: One: below a threshold frequency, nothing. A blinding red arc lamp frees no electrons from sodium. A dim ultraviolet lamp frees them instantly.
beats.tts_normalized_text: One: below a threshold frequency, nothing. A blinding red arc lamp frees no electrons from sodium. A dim ultraviolet lamp frees them instantly.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO+ANNOTATION] carbon arc lamp / searchlight plate; annotation plane adds 'RED: nothing' vs 'UV: instantly' chips at assembly — plate slots now

beats.narration_text: Two: brighter light means more electrons — but not faster ones. Double the brightness, and each electron carries exactly the same energy as before.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Two: brighter light means more electrons — but not faster ones. Double the brightness, and each electron carries exactly the same energy as before.

viz.data: isotype: one row of electron squares, then double brightness -> two rows, every square the SAME size; 'same energy each' chip
viz.note: KE independent of intensity (chapter fact two)

beats.narration_text: Three: no delay. Emission begins within nanoseconds, at any brightness above threshold. Even the dimmest lamp never needs time to charge up an electron.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Three: no delay. Emission begins within nanoseconds, at any brightness above threshold. Even the dimmest lamp never needs time to charge up an electron.

shot.note: AI-generated plate — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] gold-leaf electroscope or precision lab instrument, circa 1900; set shot.focus on the instrument

beats.narration_text: Waves can't do any of this. A wave delivers energy smoothly, everywhere at once. Given enough time, any color should shake an electron loose. None of it happens.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Waves can't do any of this. A wave delivers energy smoothly, everywhere at once. Given enough time, any color should shake an electron loose. None of it happens.

viz.data: three classical expectations as serif labels — 'any color, given time' / 'brighter means faster' / 'energy needs to accumulate' — each gets a hand-drawn terracotta strike as the narration lands
viz.note: one editor's-pen voice per graphic: the strikes ARE the voice here

beats.narration_text: Enter a twenty-six-year-old patent clerk in Bern, in his miracle year: Albert Einstein, taking the step Planck himself refused.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Enter a twenty-six-year-old patent clerk in Bern, in his miracle year: Albert Einstein, taking the step Planck himself refused.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Einstein (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Einstein at the Bern patent office era (~1905, young, seated at desk); set shot.focus on his face

beats.narration_text: Light isn't a smooth wave spread everywhere. It arrives in whole packets — Planck's chunks, now for light itself. Einstein called them light quanta. We call them photons.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Light isn't a smooth wave spread everywhere. It arrives in whole packets — Planck's chunks, now for light itself. Einstein called them light quanta. We call them photons.

viz.data: a smooth navy wave greys out; a stream of discrete terracotta dots (photons) crosses in its place; 'Lichtquanten' serif label with hairline
viz.note: Einstein 1905: quantizes the FIELD itself — the step beyond Planck (chapter)

beats.narration_text: The rules become simple. One photon is absorbed by one electron. Whole packet or nothing — a photon cannot pay in installments.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The rules become simple. One photon is absorbed by one electron. Whole packet or nothing — a photon cannot pay in installments.

viz.data: one terracotta photon dot arrives at a slate metal edge, one navy electron leaves; a second sub-threshold photon fades with an X — whole packet or nothing

beats.narration_text: One line explains every impossible fact: the electron's energy is the photon's payment, minus the exit fee. Physicists call that fee the work function.
beats.tts_normalized_text: One line explains every impossible fact: the electron's energy is the photon's payment, minus the exit fee. Physicists call that fee the work function.

tangent.rhs: The photon's payment, minus the metal's exit fee.
tangent.claim: Exactly this — each photon acts alone, whole or not at all.

example.scenario: UV at 300 nm on sodium. Fee: 2.28 eV.
example.verdict: UV: electron keeps 1.85 eV · green: 0.01 short — nothing
example.cost: Sub-threshold photons never add up. Each acts alone.

tangent.reentry: And that is the whole law. Back to the three facts.

beats.narration_text: K max, the fastest electron's energy. H nu, the photon's payment. And phi — the work function — what the metal charges to let an electron leave.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Kay max, the fastest electron's energy. H new, the photon's payment. And fie — the work function — what the metal charges to let an electron leave.

beats.narration_text: Ultraviolet on sodium: pay 4.13, fee 2.28 — the electron keeps 1.85 electron volts. Green light pays 2.27. One hundredth short. Nothing escapes. Ever.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Ultraviolet on sodium: pay 4.13, fee 2.28 — the electron keeps 1.85 electron volts. Green light pays 2.27. One hundredth short. Nothing escapes. Ever.

beats.narration_text: Threshold, explained: below the fee, no photon can pay. Faster electrons from bluer light: the payment is set by frequency alone. And no delay: one photon, one electron.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Threshold, explained: below the fee, no photon can pay. Faster electrons from bluer light: the payment is set by frequency alone. And no delay: one photon, one electron.

viz.data: the three impossible facts return as serif labels, each now getting a navy check on its narration cue — the mirror of B07's strikes

beats.narration_text: Robert Millikan thought photons were absurd. He spent two years trying to prove Einstein wrong — freshly scraped metal surfaces, vacuum, exquisite care.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Robert Millikan thought photons were absurd. He spent two years trying to prove Einstein wrong — freshly scraped metal surfaces, vacuum, exquisite care.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Millikan (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Robert Millikan portrait or at apparatus (Caltech-era acceptable); set shot.focus on his face

beats.narration_text: Every metal gave the same slope: h over e. Einstein's equation fit perfectly. Millikan published his own defeat — "in spite of my personal conviction."
beats.tts_normalized_text: Every metal gave the same slope: h over e. Einstein's equation fit perfectly. Millikan published his own defeat — "in spite of my personal conviction."

viz.data: axes: stopping potential vs frequency; three parallel navy lines (sodium, lithium, potassium) draw on with different thresholds, one crimson slope label 'h/e — the same for every metal'

beats.narration_text: Einstein's Nobel Prize came in 1921 — for the photoelectric effect, not relativity. Millikan's came two years later, for proving the theory he'd set out to kill.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Einstein's Nobel Prize came in 1921 — for the photoelectric effect, not relativity. Millikan's came two years later, for proving the theory he'd set out to kill.

viz.data: two slate cards: EINSTEIN · 1921 · 'for the photoelectric effect' / MILLIKAN · 1923 · 'for measuring h' — terracotta ring on 'not relativity' line
viz.note: Einstein Nobel 1921 for photoelectric effect; Millikan 1923 (chapter)

beats.narration_text: Who was this patent clerk? A story of its own. What he did to light — wave AND particle at once — has no classical answer.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Who was this patent clerk? A story of its own. What he did to light — wave AND particle at once — has no classical answer.

shot.note: AI-generated portrayal of Einstein (PORTRAIT preset) — synthetic, disclose in credits

beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] Einstein portrait ~1905 (young, not the elderly icon); name label + dates at assembly; bio kicker per SKILL.md

beats.narration_text: Next: if light is both wave and particle — what, exactly, is an electron?
beats.tts_normalized_text: Next: if light is both wave and particle — what, exactly, is an electron?
